school: Medinan Quranic School — one of the seven canonical qaris; his riwaya dominates in North and West Africa today
quotes: I served Ibn Umar for thirty years and observed him more carefully than any book.
The student who serves his teacher learns more than the student who only listens.
Act on what you know before you seek more knowledge.

school: The founding father of systematic hadith criticism (al-jarh wa'l-ta'dil — impugning and accrediting narrators); Ahmad ibn Hanbal said: 'I have not seen anyone like Yahya ibn Sa'id al-Qattan'; his rigorous evaluation of hadith transmitters became the methodological standard for all subsequent hadith scholarship; without him the Six Books may never have been compiled
bio: Early Islamic hadith scholar and jurist known for his expertise in hadith authentication and transmission during the 8th century.

school: Ahmad ibn Hanbal said: 'I have not seen anyone more complete in knowledge than Waki' ibn al-Jarrah'; both al-Shafi'i and Ahmad ibn Hanbal studied under him; famous for combining exceptional memory with piety; al-Shafi'i composed a poem about him after he complained of forgetting things
children: Sufyan bin Wky' bin al-Jrah , Mlyh bin Wky' bin al-Jrah , 'Ubaid bin Wky' bin al-Jrah
quotes: I have never written anything with my right hand that I was not prepared to stand before God with.
The student who seeks knowledge for God will be given more than he seeks.
Preserve the hadith — for it is the fortress of the religion.

school: Muhammad ibn Ismail al-Bukhari (810–870), compiler of Sahih al-Bukhari, universally regarded as the most authentic book after the Quran. From 600,000 hadiths he heard, he selected 7,275 (2,602 without repetition) applying the strictest verification criteria ever devised. Born in Bukhara, he travelled for sixteen years across the Islamic world collecting hadiths from over 1,000 scholars.

books.title: Sahih al-Bukhari (The Authentic Collection)
books.note: Most authentic book after the Quran according to Sunni Muslims — 7,563 narrations selected from 600,000.

quotes: I did not record a single hadith in this book until I had performed ablution, prayed two rak'ahs, and sought God's guidance.
I never recorded a single hadith in the Sahih without first performing ablution and praying two rak'ahs.
A single confirmed lie from a narrator is sufficient to cast doubt upon all his narrations.
Actions are judged by intentions — and every man shall have what he intended.
Whoever treads a path seeking knowledge, Allah will ease for him the path to Paradise.
I never recorded a hadith in this book without performing ablution and praying two rak’ahs.
I hope to meet God without any person being able to claim I have wronged them.
We are more in need of a little sincerity than a great deal of action.

school: Khorasan / Hadith Sciences — Second most authoritative hadith collection

books.title: Sahih Muslim (The Authentic Collection)
books.note: Second most authoritative hadith collection in Sunni Islam — 7,500 narrations with rigorous chain analysis.

school: Shafi'i school — greatest hadith scholar of his era; wrote the 40 Hadith al-Nawawi still memorized worldwide
quotes: The intention behind the action is more important than the action itself.
The student of knowledge should act upon what he knows — lest his knowledge stand as a witness against him.
The foundation of this religion is the Book of Allah and the Sunnah of His Prophet, upon whom be peace.
Part of the perfection of a person's Islam is leaving that which does not concern him.
The Muslim is one from whose tongue and hand other Muslims are safe.
The sincere person is one whose actions in private and in public are the same.
The heart is polished by remembrance of God and tarnished by heedlessness.
Whoever knows himself knows his Lord.

school: Shafi'i / Damascene school — Wrote the most comprehensive biography of Islamic scholars across 23 volumes
quotes: How many people have I met who memorised a thousand hadith, yet their deeds were few.
Knowledge without action is a tree without fruit — and action without knowledge is a shadow without a sun.
The scholar who does not know his own weakness has not yet begun to know himself.
The righteous scholar is one who fears God more the more he knows.
A man is known by three things: his silence, his speech, and his actions.

school: Ismail ibn Umar ibn Kathir (1300–1373), Shafi'i scholar and student of Ibn Taymiyyah, who wrote the most widely used classical Quran commentary (Tafsir Ibn Kathir), interpreting the Quran primarily through hadith. His al-Bidaya wa al-Nihaya is a monumental history from creation to his own time. Based in Damascus.
quotes: Do not be deceived by the length of time — for every moment brings you closer to your end.
The greatest of all miracles given to the Prophet was the Quran — for it is the eternal proof that endures.
History is a mirror — the wise man looks into it and sees not the past but himself.
